name: Masan
description: Masan, also known as Masaniya, is a malevolent spirit prevalent in North Indian folklore, particularly in Rajasthan and Gujarat. These spirits are believed to be the souls of children who died untimely deaths, often due to disease, accident, or neglect. Bitter and resentful of their truncated lives, Masans haunt cremation grounds, desolate places, and crossroads, seeking to torment the living. They are often depicted as being mischievous and malicious, capable of causing illness, misfortune, and even death. Masans are particularly dangerous to pregnant women and young children, whom they seek to harm or possess. Protective rituals and amulets are often employed to ward off their malevolent influence. In some traditions, Masans are associated with black magic and are invoked by sorcerers to carry out evil deeds. Their existence highlights cultural anxieties surrounding infant mortality and the unresolved grief associated with the loss of young lives, reflecting a belief in the lingering power of the unfulfilled spirit.
subtitle: Restless spirit of deceased children
appearance: Masans are often described as having a grotesque appearance, reflecting their unnatural state of existence. They may appear as shadowy figures, distorted children, or even take on the form of animals. Their eyes are said to glow with an eerie light, and their skin is often described as being pale or decaying. Some accounts depict them as having elongated limbs and sharp claws.
habitat: Cremation grounds, desolate places, crossroads, abandoned buildings, and areas where children have died.
behavior: Masans are primarily driven by resentment and a desire to inflict suffering on the living. They are mischievous and malicious, often targeting pregnant women and young children. They may cause illness, induce nightmares, and even possess individuals. They are said to be particularly active during the night and are drawn to places where death and sorrow are present.

keyMyths.title: The Masan and the Pregnant Woman
keyMyths.description: A pregnant woman unknowingly wanders near a cremation ground and is attacked by a Masan. She is saved by a protective amulet given to her by a wise woman.

keyMyths.title: The Masan's Deception
keyMyths.description: A Masan shapeshifts into a child's form to lure unsuspecting villagers into a trap. A local priest recognizes the deception and banishes the Masan with sacred chants.

sources.author: William Crooke
sources.work: The Popular Religion and Folk-Lore of Northern India
sources.description: Provides detailed descriptions of various spirits and deities in North Indian folklore.
sources.text: Provides detailed descriptions of various spirits and deities in North Indian folklore.

sources.author: Verrier Elwin
sources.work: The Tribal Art of Middle India
sources.description: Documents beliefs surrounding spirits in tribal communities.
sources.text: Documents beliefs surrounding spirits in tribal communities.
